description_long: This object is a tree stump with a roughly cylindrical trunk that has been cut horizontally at the top, revealing concentric growth rings. The bark is textured and dark brown, with subtle vertical ridges and irregularities, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. At the base, several thick roots extend outward in different directions, some partially above ground, adding to the organic form. The top surface is lighter in color, with visible wood grain and a slightly uneven edge where the trunk was cut. The stump appears solid and robust, typical of a mature tree that has been felled. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural seat or obstacle in an urban park or green space.
description: A weathered tree stump with textured bark, exposed roots, and a cut top showing growth rings. The stump is solid, brown, and natural-looking, suitable for parks or green areas.
description_view_0: The stump is seen with three prominent roots extending outward, and the cut top is clearly visible with growth rings.
description_view_1: This view highlights the right side with two main roots and a slightly slanted top surface, showing more of the bark texture.
description_view_2: The back of the stump is visible, with fewer roots extending outward and a smoother bark surface.
description_view_3: The left side is shown, with two roots visible and the top cut surface partially obscured.
